    64→
    65→
    66→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    67→class LandTarget:
    68→    """Resolved landing target from any entry point.
    69→
    70→    Carries all resolved state needed to land a PR, regardless of how
    71→    the target was specified (current branch, PR number, or branch name).
    72→    """
    73→
    74→    branch: str
    75→    pr_details: PRDetails
    76→    worktree_path: Path | None
    77→    is_current_branch: bool
    78→    use_graphite: bool
    79→    target_child_branch: str | None  # Only set for --up mode
    80→
    81→
    82→@dataclass(frozen=True)
    83→class CleanupContext:
    84→    """Carries cleanup state through the extraction process.
    85→
    86→    This dataclass bundles all parameters needed for cleanup operations,
    87→    enabling focused helper functions without parameter explosion.
    88→    """
    89→
    90→    ctx: ErkContext
    91→    repo: RepoContext
    92→    branch: str
    93→    worktree_path: Path | None
    94→    main_repo_root: Path
    95→    script: bool
    96→    pull_flag: bool
    97→    force: bool
    98→    is_current_branch: bool
    99→    target_child_branch: str | None
   100→    no_delete: bool
   101→    skip_activation_output: bool
   102→    cleanup_confirmed: bool  # Pre-gathered from validation phase
   103→
   104→
   105→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   106→class CleanupConfirmation:
   107→    """Pre-gathered cleanup confirmation result.
   108→
   109→    Captures user's response to cleanup prompt during validation phase,
   110→    allowing all confirmations to be batched upfront before any mutations.
   111→    """
   112→
   113→    proceed: bool  # True = proceed with cleanup, False = preserve
   114→
   115→
   116→class CleanupType(Enum):
   117→    """Classification of cleanup scenario after PR merge."""
   118→
   119→    NO_DELETE = auto()
   120→    NO_WORKTREE = auto()
   121→    SLOT_ASSIGNED = auto()
   122→    SLOT_UNASSIGNED = auto()
   123→    NON_SLOT = auto()
   124→
   125→
   126→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   127→class ResolvedCleanup:
   128→    """Result of classifying the cleanup scenario.
   129→
   130→    Carries the enum variant plus optional resolved state for the
   131→    SLOT_ASSIGNED variant (avoids re-resolving pool state after classification).
   132→    """
   133→
   134→    cleanup_type: CleanupType
   135→    pool_state: PoolState | None
   136→    assignment: SlotAssignment | None
   137→
   138→
   139→def determine_cleanup_type(
   140→    *,
   141→    no_delete: bool,
   142→    worktree_path: Path | None,
   143→    pool_json_path: Path,
   144→    branch: str,
   145→) -> ResolvedCleanup:
   146→    """Classify the cleanup scenario for a landed branch.
   147→
   148→    Mirrors the dispatch logic in _cleanup_and_navigate but returns an explicit
   149→    enum variant instead of branching inline. Pure classification — no side effects.
   150→    """
   151→    if no_delete:
   152→        return ResolvedCleanup(
   153→            cleanup_type=CleanupType.NO_DELETE,
   154→            pool_state=None,
   155→            assignment=None,
   156→        )
   157→
   158→    if worktree_path is None:
   159→        return ResolvedCleanup(
   160→            cleanup_type=CleanupType.NO_WORKTREE,
   161→            pool_state=None,
   162→            assignment=None,
   163→        )
   164→
   165→    state = load_pool_state(pool_json_path)
   166→    assignment = find_branch_assignment(state, branch) if state is not None else None
   167→
   168→    if assignment is not None:
   169→        return ResolvedCleanup(
   170→            cleanup_type=CleanupType.SLOT_ASSIGNED,
   171→            pool_state=state,
   172→            assignment=assignment,
   173→        )
   174→
   175→    if extract_slot_number(worktree_path.name) is not None:
   176→        return ResolvedCleanup(
   177→            cleanup_type=CleanupType.SLOT_UNASSIGNED,
   178→            pool_state=state,
   179→            assignment=None,
   180→        )
   181→
   182→    return ResolvedCleanup(
   183→        cleanup_type=CleanupType.NON_SLOT,
   184→        pool_state=state,
   185→        assignment=None,
   186→    )
   187→
   188→
   189→def _gather_cleanup_confirmation(
   190→    ctx: ErkContext,
   191→    *,
   192→    target: LandTarget,
   193→    repo: RepoContext,
   194→    force: bool,
   195→) -> CleanupConfirmation:
   196→    """Gather cleanup confirmation upfront during validation.
   197→
   198→    Uses determine_cleanup_type() to classify the cleanup scenario,
   199→    then prompts based on the classification. Returns result for
   200→    threading through to cleanup functions.
   201→
   202→    This consolidates all cleanup-related confirmation prompts into
   203→    the validation phase, before any mutations occur.
   204→    """
   205→    if force or ctx.dry_run:
   206→        return CleanupConfirmation(proceed=True)
   207→
   208→    resolved = determine_cleanup_type(
   209→        no_delete=False,  # no_delete handled separately in _handle_no_delete
   210→        worktree_path=target.worktree_path,
   211→        pool_json_path=repo.pool_json_path,
   212→        branch=target.branch,
   213→    )
   214→
   215→    match resolved.cleanup_type:
   216→        case CleanupType.NO_DELETE | CleanupType.NO_WORKTREE:
   217→            # No confirmation needed
   218→            return CleanupConfirmation(proceed=True)
   219→        case CleanupType.SLOT_ASSIGNED:
   220→            assert resolved.assignment is not None
   221→            proceed = ctx.console.confirm(
   222→                f"After landing, unassign slot '{resolved.assignment.slot_name}' "
   223→                f"and delete branch '{target.branch}'?",
   224→                default=True,
   225→            )
   226→        case CleanupType.SLOT_UNASSIGNED:
   227→            assert target.worktree_path is not None
   228→            user_output(
   229→                click.style("Warning:", fg="yellow")
   230→                + f" Slot '{target.worktree_path.name}' has no assignment "
   231→                + "(branch checked out outside erk)."
   232→            )
   233→            user_output("  Use `erk pr co` or `erk branch checkout` to track slot usage.")
   234→            proceed = ctx.console.confirm(
   235→                f"After landing, release slot '{target.worktree_path.name}' "
   236→                f"and delete branch '{target.branch}'?",
   237→                default=True,
   238→            )
   239→        case CleanupType.NON_SLOT:
   240→            assert target.worktree_path is not None
   241→            proceed = ctx.console.confirm(
   242→                f"After landing, delete branch '{target.branch}'"
   243→                f" and remove worktree '{target.worktree_path.name}'?",
   244→                default=True,
   245→            )
   246→        case _:
   247→            assert_never(resolved.cleanup_type)
   248→
   249→    return CleanupConfirmation(proceed=proceed)
   250→
   251→
   252→def _check_learn_status_and_prompt(
   253→    ctx: ErkContext,
   254→    *,
   255→    repo_root: Path,
   256→    plan_id: str,
   257→    force: bool,
   258→    script: bool,
   259→) -> None:
   260→    """Check if plan has been learned from and prompt user if not.
   261→
   262→    This provides a conservative check before landing - if the plan has associated
   263→    sessions but hasn't been learned from yet, warn the user and give them the
   264→    option to cancel, trigger async learn, or continue without learning.
   265→
   266→    Learn plans (issues with erk-learn label) are skipped since they are for
   267→    extracting insights, not for being "learned from" themselves.
   268→
   269→    Args:
   270→        ctx: ErkContext
   271→        repo_root: Repository root path
   272→        plan_id: Plan identifier string
   273→        force: If True, skip the check entirely
   274→        script: If True, output no-op activation script on abort
   275→
   276→    Raises:
   277→        SystemExit(0) if user declines to continue
   278→    """
   279→    if force:
   280→        return
   281→
   282→    # Check override chain: local_config overrides global_config
   283→    # local_config contains merged repo+local values (local wins over repo)
   284→    if ctx.local_config.prompt_learn_on_land is not None:
   285→        # Repo or local level override exists
   286→        if not ctx.local_config.prompt_learn_on_land:
   287→            return
   288→    elif ctx.global_config is not None and not ctx.global_config.prompt_learn_on_land:
   289→        # Fall back to global config
   290→        return
   291→
   292→    # Skip learn check for learn plans (they don't need to be learned from)
   293→    plan_result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo_root, plan_id)
   294→    if isinstance(plan_result, PlanNotFound):
   295→        user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Issue #{plan_id} not found")
   296→        return
   297→    if "erk-learn" in plan_result.labels:
   298→        return
   299→
   300→    # Check learn_status from plan header metadata
   301→    learn_status = ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, "learn_status")
   302→    if isinstance(learn_status, PlanNotFound):
   303→        return
   304→
   305→    # Handle completed statuses - learn has already finished
   306→    completed_statuses = {"completed_no_plan", "completed_with_plan", "plan_completed"}
   307→    if learn_status in completed_statuses:
   308→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" Learn completed for plan #{plan_id}")
   309→        return
   310→
   311→    # Handle pending status - async learn is in progress
   312→    if learn_status == "pending":
   313→        user_output(click.style("⏳", fg="cyan") + f" Async learn in progress for plan #{plan_id}")
   314→        return
   315→
   316→    # learn_status is null or not_started - fall through to check sessions
   317→
   318→    # Check for existing learn sessions (backward compatibility)
   319→    plan_issue_number = int(plan_id)
   320→    sessions = ctx.plan_backend.find_sessions_for_plan(repo_root, plan_id)
   321→
   322→    if sessions.learn_session_ids:
   323→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" Learn completed for plan #{plan_id}")
   324→        return
   325→
   326→    # No learn has happened - prompt user for action
   327→    _prompt_async_learn_and_continue(
   328→        ctx,
   329→        repo_root=repo_root,
   330→        plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number,
   331→        script=script,
   332→    )
   333→
   334→
   335→def _prompt_async_learn_and_continue(
   336→    ctx: ErkContext,
   337→    *,
   338→    repo_root: Path,
   339→    plan_issue_number: int,
   340→    script: bool,
   341→) -> None:
   342→    """Prompt user for async learn options when no learning has occurred.
   343→
   344→    Offers four choices:
   345→    1. Trigger async learn and continue - dispatches workflow, then lands
   346→    2. Continue without learning - proceeds with landing
   347→    3. Cancel - aborts landing
   348→    4. Preprocess sessions and continue - uploads gist, then lands
   349→
   350→    Args:
   351→        ctx: ErkContext
   352→        repo_root: Repository root path
   353→        plan_issue_number: Issue number of the plan
   354→        script: If True, output no-op activation script on abort
   355→
   356→    Raises:
   357→        SystemExit(0) if user cancels
   358→    """
   359→    user_output(
   360→        "⚠️  " + click.style(f"Plan #{plan_issue_number} has not been learned from.", fg="yellow")
   361→    )
   362→    user_output("")
   363→
   364→    # In script mode, auto-select "trigger async learn" as the default action
   365→    if not ctx.console.is_stdin_interactive():
   366→        _trigger_async_learn(ctx, repo_root=repo_root, plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number)
   367→        return
   368→
   369→    # Interactive mode: show numbered choice menu
   370→    user_output("Choose an action:")
   371→    user_output("  1. 🚀 Trigger async learn and continue (recommended)")
   372→    user_output("     Reads session logs, commits to learn branch, runs analysis in GitHub Actions")
   373→    user_output("  2. ⏩ Continue without learning")
   374→    user_output("  3. ❌ Cancel")
   375→    user_output("  4. 📖 Preprocess sessions, then continue landing")
   376→    user_output(
   377→        f"     Commits learn branch so you can run `erk learn {plan_issue_number}` at your leisure"
   378→    )
   379→    user_output("")
   380→
   381→    choice = click.prompt(
   382→        "Enter choice",
   383→        type=click.IntRange(1, 4),
   384→        default=1,
   385→    )
   386→
   387→    if choice == 1:
   388→        # Trigger async learn workflow
   389→        _trigger_async_learn(ctx, repo_root=repo_root, plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number)
   390→    elif choice == 2:
   391→        # Continue without learning
   392→        user_output("Continuing without learning.")
   393→    elif choice == 3:
   394→        # Cancel
   395→        user_output("Cancelled. Run 'erk learn' first, then retry landing.")
   396→        if script:
   397→            script_content = render_activation_script(
   398→                worktree_path=ctx.cwd,
   399→                target_subpath=None,
   400→                post_cd_commands=None,
   401→                final_message='echo "Cancelled - run erk learn first"',
   402→                comment="land cancelled (learn check)",
   403→            )
   404→            result = ctx.script_writer.write_activation_script(
   405→                script_content,
   406→                command_name="land",
   407→                comment="cancelled",
   408→            )
   409→            machine_output(str(result.path), nl=False)
   410→        raise SystemExit(0)
   411→    else:
   412→        # Preprocess and manual learn - run preprocessing, store gist URL, print command and exit
   413→        _preprocess_and_prepare_manual_learn(
   414→            ctx,
   415→            repo_root=repo_root,
   416→            plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number,
   417→        )
   418→
   419→
   420→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   421→class LearnPreprocessResult:
   422→    """Successful result from learn preprocessing subprocess."""
   423→
   424→    output: dict[str, object]
   425→    learn_branch: str | None
   426→
   427→
   428→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   429→class LearnPreprocessError:
   430→    """Error from learn preprocessing subprocess."""
   431→
   432→    message: str
   433→
   434→
   435→def _run_learn_preprocessing(
   436→    ctx: ErkContext,
   437→    *,
   438→    plan_issue_number: int,
   439→    skip_workflow: bool,
   440→) -> LearnPreprocessResult | LearnPreprocessError:
   441→    """Run learn preprocessing subprocess and store learn branch on plan header.
   442→
   443→    Calls `erk exec trigger-async-learn` with optional --skip-workflow flag,
   444→    parses the JSON output, and stores the learn branch on the plan issue header
   445→    if preprocessing was successful.
   446→
   447→    Args:
   448→        ctx: ErkContext
   449→        plan_issue_number: Issue number of the plan
   450→        skip_workflow: If True, run preprocessing only (no CI workflow trigger)
   451→
   452→    Returns:
   453→        LearnPreprocessResult on success, LearnPreprocessError on failure
   454→    """
   455→    cmd = ["erk", "exec", "trigger-async-learn", str(plan_issue_number)]
   456→    if skip_workflow:
   457→        cmd.append("--skip-workflow")
   458→
   459→    try:
   460→        # Stream stderr (progress) in real-time while capturing stdout (JSON result)
   461→        process = subprocess.Popen(
   462→            cmd,
   463→            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   464→            stderr=None,  # Inherit stderr - progress streams through
   465→            text=True,
   466→            cwd=ctx.cwd,
   467→        )
   468→        stdout, _ = process.communicate()
   469→    except FileNotFoundError:
   470→        return LearnPreprocessError(message="erk command not found")
   471→
   472→    if process.returncode != 0:
   473→        return LearnPreprocessError(message=_parse_trigger_error(stdout, ""))
   474→
   475→    # Parse output JSON
   476→    output = json.loads(stdout)
   477→    learn_branch = output.get("learn_branch")
   478→
   479→    # Store learn branch on plan header for auto-detection by `erk learn`
   480→    if learn_branch and output.get("success"):
   481→        _store_learn_materials_branch(
   482→            ctx,
   483→            repo_root=ctx.cwd,
   484→            plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number,
   485→            learn_branch=learn_branch,
   486→        )
   487→
   488→    return LearnPreprocessResult(output=output, learn_branch=learn_branch)
   489→
   490→
   491→def _trigger_async_learn(
   492→    ctx: ErkContext,
   493→    *,
   494→    repo_root: Path,
   495→    plan_issue_number: int,
   496→) -> None:
   497→    """Trigger async learn workflow for a plan issue.
   498→
   499→    Calls `erk exec trigger-async-learn` via subprocess and reports result.
   500→    This is a fire-and-forget operation - landing continues immediately.
   501→
   502→    Args:
   503→        ctx: ErkContext
   504→        repo_root: Repository root path (unused, for consistency)
   505→        plan_issue_number: Issue number of the plan
   506→    """
   507→    # Silence unused parameter warning - repo_root kept for interface consistency
   508→    _ = repo_root
   509→
   510→    header = click.style(f"🧠 Triggering async learn for plan #{plan_issue_number}...", fg="cyan")
   511→    user_output(header)
   512→    user_output("   Reads local session logs, preprocesses them, and commits to a learn branch.")
   513→    user_output(
   514→        "   A GitHub Actions workflow then analyzes sessions and creates a documentation plan."
   515→    )
   516→    user_output("")
   517→
   518→    result = _run_learn_preprocessing(ctx, plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number, skip_workflow=False)
   519→
   520→    if isinstance(result, LearnPreprocessError):
   521→        user_output(
   522→            click.style("⚠ ", fg="yellow") + f"Could not trigger async learn: {result.message}"
   523→        )
   524→        return
   525→
   526→    output = result.output
   527→    if output.get("success"):
   528→        workflow_url = output.get("workflow_url", "")
   529→        if workflow_url:
   530→            user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" Async learn triggered: {workflow_url}")
   531→        else:
   532→            # Fallback for backwards compatibility
   533→            user_output(
   534→                click.style("✓", fg="green")
   535→                + f" Async learn triggered (run: {output.get('run_id', 'unknown')})"
   536→            )
   537→        user_output(
   538→            f"  Results will appear as a new GitHub issue linked to plan #{plan_issue_number}."
   539→        )
   540→    else:
   541→        user_output(
   542→            click.style("⚠ ", fg="yellow")
   543→            + f"Async learn response: {output.get('error', 'unknown error')}"
   544→        )
   545→
   546→
   547→def _parse_trigger_error(stdout: str, stderr: str) -> str:
   548→    """Parse error message from trigger-async-learn failure.
   549→
   550→    Attempts to extract error from JSON stdout, falls back to stderr or stdout.
   551→    """
   552→    # Check if stdout looks like JSON (starts with {) before parsing
   553→    if stdout and stdout.strip().startswith("{"):
   554→        try:
   555→            error_output = json.loads(stdout)
   556→            error = error_output.get("error")
   557→            if error:
   558→                return error
   559→        except json.JSONDecodeError:
   560→            pass  # Fall through to return raw stderr/stdout
   561→    return stderr or stdout or "unknown error"
   562→

   564→def _store_learn_materials_branch(
   565→    ctx: ErkContext,
   566→    *,
   567→    repo_root: Path,
   568→    plan_issue_number: int,
   569→    learn_branch: str,
   570→) -> None:
   571→    """Store learn materials branch on the plan's metadata.
   572→
   573→    Updates the learn_materials_branch field on the plan-header.
   574→    Errors are logged but do not propagate.
   575→
   576→    Args:
   577→        ctx: ErkContext
   578→        repo_root: Repository root path
   579→        plan_issue_number: Issue number of the plan
   580→        learn_branch: Name of the branch containing learn materials
   581→    """
   582→    plan_id = str(plan_issue_number)
   583→    try:
   584→        ctx.plan_backend.update_metadata(
   585→            repo_root, plan_id, {"learn_materials_branch": learn_branch}
   586→        )
   587→    except PlanHeaderNotFoundError:
   588→        # No metadata block — store as comment instead
   589→        try:
   590→            ctx.plan_backend.add_comment(
   591→                repo_root, plan_id, f"Learn materials branch: {learn_branch}"
   592→            )
   593→        except RuntimeError as comment_err:
   594→            user_output(
   595→                click.style("⚠ ", fg="yellow")
   596→                + f"Could not store learn branch on plan {plan_id}: {comment_err}"
   597→            )
   598→    except RuntimeError as e:
   599→        user_output(
   600→            click.style("⚠ ", fg="yellow") + f"Could not store learn branch on plan {plan_id}: {e}"
   601→        )
   602→

   604→def _preprocess_and_prepare_manual_learn(
   605→    ctx: ErkContext,
   606→    *,
   607→    repo_root: Path,
   608→    plan_issue_number: int,
   609→) -> None:
   610→    """Run preprocessing for manual learn, store branch on plan, then continue landing.
   611→
   612→    Calls `erk exec trigger-async-learn --skip-workflow` to do full preprocessing
   613→    (discover sessions, preprocess XML, fetch PR comments, commit to learn branch)
   614→    without triggering the CI workflow. Then stores the branch on the plan issue header.
   615→
   616→    After preprocessing (success or failure), returns to continue with landing.
   617→    The user can run `erk learn` at their leisure.
   618→
   619→    Args:
   620→        ctx: ErkContext
   621→        repo_root: Repository root path (unused, for interface consistency)
   622→        plan_issue_number: Issue number of the plan
   623→    """
   624→    _ = repo_root
   625→
   626→    header = click.style(f"🔄 Preprocessing sessions for plan #{plan_issue_number}...", fg="cyan")
   627→    user_output(header)
   628→    user_output("   Reads local session logs, preprocesses them, and commits to a learn branch.")
   629→    user_output("")
   630→
   631→    result = _run_learn_preprocessing(ctx, plan_issue_number=plan_issue_number, skip_workflow=True)
   632→
   633→    if isinstance(result, LearnPreprocessError):
   634→        user_output(
   635→            click.style("⚠ ", fg="yellow")
   636→            + f"Preprocessing failed: {result.message}. "
   637→            + f"Run manually: erk learn {plan_issue_number}"
   638→        )
   639→        user_output("Continuing with landing.")
   640→        return
   641→
   642→    if result.learn_branch:
   643→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Preprocessed and committed learn materials")
   644→
   645→    user_output(f"Run `erk learn {plan_issue_number}` at your leisure to learn from this plan.")
   646→    user_output("Continuing with landing.")
   647→
