    93→def test_cleanup_and_navigate_detects_slot_by_branch_name(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    94→    """Test that slot detection uses branch name, not worktree path.
    95→
    96→    Regression test for bug where slot worktrees were not detected because
    97→    path comparison failed. The fix uses find_branch_assignment() instead of
    98→    find_assignment_by_worktree_path() to match by branch name.
    99→    """
   100→    # Use different paths to simulate path mismatch scenario
   101→    # In the bug, pool.json stored one path but git reported a different path
   102→    stored_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-a" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
   103→    actual_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-b" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
   104→    stored_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   105→    actual_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   106→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
   107→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   108→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   109→    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"
   110→
   111→    # Create pool state with assignment using stored path (different from actual)
   112→    assignment = _create_test_assignment(
   113→        slot_name="erk-slot-01",
   114→        branch_name="feature-branch",
   115→        worktree_path=stored_worktree_path,  # Stored path differs from actual
   116→    )
   117→
   118→    initial_state = PoolState.test(assignments=(assignment,))
   119→    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, initial_state)
   120→
   121→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   122→        worktrees={
   123→            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=actual_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
   124→        },
   125→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   126→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   127→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
   128→        existing_paths={
   129→            actual_worktree_path,
   130→            stored_worktree_path,  # Assignment uses this path
   131→            main_repo_root,
   132→            main_repo_root / ".git",
   133→            pool_json_path,
   134→        },
   135→    )
   136→
   137→    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
   138→    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
   139→    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
   140→        branches={
   141→            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
   142→                name="feature-branch",
   143→                parent="main",
   144→                children=[],
   145→                is_trunk=False,
   146→                commit_sha=None,
   147→            ),
   148→        },
   149→    )
   150→
   151→    ctx = context_for_test(
   152→        git=fake_git,
   153→        graphite=fake_graphite,
   154→        cwd=actual_worktree_path,
   155→    )
   156→
   157→    repo = RepoContext(
   158→        root=main_repo_root,
   159→        repo_name="test-repo",
   160→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   161→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   162→        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
   163→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   164→    )
   165→
   166→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with the actual path (which differs from stored)
   167→    # The bug would cause this to NOT detect the slot and delete the worktree
   168→    # The fix should detect the slot by branch name and unassign instead
   169→    try:
   170→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   171→            ctx=ctx,
   172→            repo=repo,
   173→            branch="feature-branch",
   174→            worktree_path=actual_worktree_path,  # Actual path differs from stored
   175→            script=False,
   176→            pull_flag=False,
   177→            force=True,
   178→            is_current_branch=False,
   179→            target_child_branch=None,
   180→            no_delete=False,
   181→            skip_activation_output=False,
   182→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   183→        )
   184→    except SystemExit:
   185→        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end
   186→
   187→    # Verify slot was unassigned (detected as slot by branch name)
   188→    reloaded_state = load_pool_state(pool_json_path)
   189→    assert reloaded_state is not None
   190→    # Assignment should be removed (slot unassigned)
   191→    matching_assignments = [
   192→        a for a in reloaded_state.assignments if a.branch_name == "feature-branch"
   193→    ]
   194→    assert len(matching_assignments) == 0, "Slot should have been unassigned"
   195→
   196→    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
   197→    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
   198→    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
   199→    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches
   200→

   202→def test_cleanup_and_navigate_detects_slot_by_path_pattern_without_assignment(
   203→    tmp_path: Path,
   204→) -> None:
   205→    """Test slot detection by worktree path pattern when no pool assignment exists.
   206→
   207→    Regression test for bug where slot worktrees (e.g., erk-slot-01) were deleted
   208→    when branches were checked out via 'gt get' instead of erk commands.
   209→
   210→    The bug occurred because:
   211→    1. 'gt get' checks out a branch without creating a pool.json assignment
   212→    2. _cleanup_and_navigate only checked pool.json for slot detection
   213→    3. With no assignment, it fell through to "delete worktree" path
   214→
   215→    The fix adds a fallback: if no assignment but worktree path matches erk-slot-XX
   216→    pattern, treat it as a slot and release (don't delete the worktree directory).
   217→    """
   218→    # Create a slot worktree without any pool assignment
   219→    slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
   220→    slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   221→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
   222→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   223→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   224→    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"
   225→
   226→    # Create empty pool state (no assignments)
   227→
   228→    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
   229→    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)
   230→
   231→    # Create the placeholder branch that should exist
   232→    placeholder_branch = "__erk-slot-01-br-stub__"
   233→
   234→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   235→        worktrees={
   236→            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
   237→        },
   238→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   239→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   240→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch", placeholder_branch]},
   241→        existing_paths={
   242→            slot_worktree_path,
   243→            main_repo_root,
   244→            main_repo_root / ".git",
   245→            pool_json_path,
   246→        },
   247→    )
   248→
   249→    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
   250→    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
   251→    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
   252→        branches={
   253→            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
   254→                name="feature-branch",
   255→                parent="main",
   256→                children=[],
   257→                is_trunk=False,
   258→                commit_sha=None,
   259→            ),
   260→        },
   261→    )
   262→
   263→    ctx = context_for_test(
   264→        git=fake_git,
   265→        graphite=fake_graphite,
   266→        cwd=slot_worktree_path,
   267→    )
   268→
   269→    repo = RepoContext(
   270→        root=main_repo_root,
   271→        repo_name="test-repo",
   272→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   273→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   274→        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
   275→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   276→    )
   277→
   278→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
   279→    # Without the fix: would delete the worktree (bad!)
   280→    # With the fix: should detect slot by path pattern and release it (good!)
   281→    try:
   282→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   283→            ctx=ctx,
   284→            repo=repo,
   285→            branch="feature-branch",
   286→            worktree_path=slot_worktree_path,
   287→            script=False,
   288→            pull_flag=False,
   289→            force=True,
   290→            is_current_branch=False,
   291→            target_child_branch=None,
   292→            no_delete=False,
   293→            skip_activation_output=False,
   294→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   295→        )
   296→    except SystemExit:
   297→        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end
   298→
   299→    # Verify worktree was NOT deleted (key assertion!)
   300→    # The worktree should still exist in git's worktree list
   301→    assert slot_worktree_path not in fake_git.removed_worktrees
   302→
   303→    # Verify placeholder branch was checked out
   304→    checkout_calls = [
   305→        (path, branch)
   306→        for path, branch in fake_git.checked_out_branches
   307→        if branch == placeholder_branch
   308→    ]
   309→    assert len(checkout_calls) == 1, "Should have checked out placeholder branch"
   310→    assert checkout_calls[0][0] == slot_worktree_path
   311→
   312→    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
   313→    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
   314→    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
   315→    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches
   316→

   318→def test_cleanup_and_navigate_non_slot_worktree_removes_worktree_after_branch_deletion(
   319→    tmp_path: Path,
   320→) -> None:
   321→    """Test that non-slot worktree cleanup removes the worktree after deleting the branch.
   322→
   323→    Non-slot worktrees have no useful state after branch deletion (unlike slot
   324→    worktrees which have placeholder branches). The worktree should be removed
   325→    entirely to avoid leaving a dead detached-HEAD directory.
   326→    """
   327→    # Create a non-slot worktree (name doesn't match erk-slot-XX pattern)
   328→    non_slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "my-feature-worktree"
   329→    non_slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   330→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
   331→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   332→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   333→    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"
   334→
   335→    # Create empty pool state (no slot assignments)
   336→
   337→    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
   338→    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)
   339→
   340→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   341→        worktrees={
   342→            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=non_slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
   343→        },
   344→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   345→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   346→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
   347→        existing_paths={
   348→            non_slot_worktree_path,
   349→            main_repo_root,
   350→            main_repo_root / ".git",
   351→            pool_json_path,
   352→        },
   353→    )
   354→
   355→    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
   356→    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
   357→    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
   358→        branches={
   359→            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
   360→                name="feature-branch",
   361→                parent="main",
   362→                children=[],
   363→                is_trunk=False,
   364→                commit_sha=None,
   365→            ),
   366→        },
   367→    )
   368→
   369→    ctx = context_for_test(
   370→        git=fake_git,
   371→        graphite=fake_graphite,
   372→        cwd=non_slot_worktree_path,
   373→    )
   374→
   375→    repo = RepoContext(
   376→        root=main_repo_root,
   377→        repo_name="test-repo",
   378→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   379→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   380→        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
   381→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   382→    )
   383→
   384→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
   385→    # Without the fix: would fail because branch is checked out
   386→    # With the fix: should checkout trunk first, then delete branch
   387→    try:
   388→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   389→            ctx=ctx,
   390→            repo=repo,
   391→            branch="feature-branch",
   392→            worktree_path=non_slot_worktree_path,
   393→            script=False,
   394→            pull_flag=False,
   395→            force=True,
   396→            is_current_branch=False,
   397→            target_child_branch=None,
   398→            no_delete=False,
   399→            skip_activation_output=False,
   400→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   401→        )
   402→    except SystemExit:
   403→        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end
   404→
   405→    # Verify detached HEAD at trunk was checked out before deletion
   406→    # (we use detached HEAD because trunk may be checked out in root worktree)
   407→    detached_calls = [(path, ref) for path, ref in fake_git.detached_checkouts if ref == "main"]
   408→    assert len(detached_calls) == 1, "Should have checked out detached HEAD at trunk"
   409→    assert detached_calls[0][0] == non_slot_worktree_path
   410→
   411→    # Verify worktree WAS removed (non-slot worktrees are removed after branch deletion)
   412→    assert non_slot_worktree_path in fake_git.removed_worktrees
   413→
   414→    # Verify safe_chdir was called to escape the worktree before removal
   415→    assert len(fake_git.chdir_history) > 0, "Should have called safe_chdir before removal"
   416→
   417→    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
   418→    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
   419→    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
   420→    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches
   421→

   506→def test_cleanup_ensures_branch_not_checked_out_before_delete_with_stale_pool_state(
   507→    tmp_path: Path,
   508→) -> None:
   509→    """Test that cleanup verifies branch is released before deletion.
   510→
   511→    Regression test for bug where delete fails when pool state's worktree_path
   512→    is stale (doesn't match the actual worktree location).
   513→
   514→    Scenario:
   515→    - Pool state has assignment with worktree_path = stale_path
   516→    - Branch is actually checked out in actual_path (different from stale_path)
   517→    - execute_unassign() checkouts placeholder at stale_path (wrong location)
   518→    - Without fix: delete_branch() fails because branch still checked out in actual_path
   519→    - With fix: _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() detects and releases the branch
   520→
   521→    The fix adds a defensive check that finds the branch wherever it's checked out
   522→    and releases it before deletion.
   523→    """
   524→    # Two different paths to simulate stale pool state
   525→    stale_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-stale" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
   526→    actual_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-actual" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
   527→    stale_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   528→    actual_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   529→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
   530→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   531→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   532→    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"
   533→
   534→    # Create pool state with assignment using STALE path (different from actual)
   535→    assignment = _create_test_assignment(
   536→        slot_name="erk-slot-01",
   537→        branch_name="feature-branch",
   538→        worktree_path=stale_worktree_path,  # STALE - differs from actual
   539→    )
   540→
   541→    initial_state = PoolState.test(assignments=(assignment,))
   542→    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, initial_state)
   543→
   544→    # Create FakeGit where branch is checked out at ACTUAL path
   545→    # This simulates the stale pool state scenario
   546→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   547→        worktrees={
   548→            main_repo_root: [
   549→                WorktreeInfo(path=actual_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
   550→            ]
   551→        },
   552→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   553→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   554→        trunk_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   555→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
   556→        existing_paths={
   557→            actual_worktree_path,
   558→            stale_worktree_path,
   559→            main_repo_root,
   560→            main_repo_root / ".git",
   561→            pool_json_path,
   562→        },
   563→    )
   564→
   565→    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch
   566→    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
   567→        branches={
   568→            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
   569→                name="feature-branch",
   570→                parent="main",
   571→                children=[],
   572→                is_trunk=False,
   573→                commit_sha=None,
   574→            ),
   575→        },
   576→    )
   577→
   578→    ctx = context_for_test(
   579→        git=fake_git,
   580→        graphite=fake_graphite,
   581→        cwd=actual_worktree_path,
   582→    )
   583→
   584→    repo = RepoContext(
   585→        root=main_repo_root,
   586→        repo_name="test-repo",
   587→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   588→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   589→        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
   590→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   591→    )
   592→
   593→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
   594→    # The bug would have failed here because branch is still checked out in actual_path
   595→    # The fix ensures branch is released before deletion
   596→    try:
   597→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   598→            ctx=ctx,
   599→            repo=repo,
   600→            branch="feature-branch",
   601→            worktree_path=actual_worktree_path,
   602→            script=False,
   603→            pull_flag=False,
   604→            force=True,
   605→            is_current_branch=False,
   606→            target_child_branch=None,
   607→            no_delete=False,
   608→            skip_activation_output=False,
   609→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   610→        )
   611→    except SystemExit:
   612→        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end
   613→
   614→    # Verify the defensive checkout_detached was called on ACTUAL path
   615→    # (This is the key assertion - the fix finds where branch is actually checked out)
   616→    detached_calls = [(path, ref) for path, ref in fake_git.detached_checkouts if ref == "main"]
   617→    # Should have at least one detached checkout at actual_worktree_path
   618→    actual_path_detached = [
   619→        (path, ref)
   620→        for path, ref in detached_calls
   621→        if path.resolve() == actual_worktree_path.resolve()
   622→    ]
   623→    assert len(actual_path_detached) >= 1, (
   624→        f"Expected detached checkout at actual_worktree_path. "
   625→        f"Got detached_checkouts: {fake_git.detached_checkouts}"
   626→    )
   627→
   628→    # Verify branch was deleted successfully
   629→    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
   630→    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches
   631→

   633→def test_ensure_branch_not_checked_out_helper_releases_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
   634→    """Test that _ensure_branch_not_checked_out helper correctly releases a branch.
   635→
   636→    This tests the helper function directly to verify it:
   637→    1. Finds the worktree where the branch is checked out
   638→    2. Checkouts detached HEAD at trunk to release the branch
   639→    3. Returns the path where detachment happened
   640→    """
   641→    repo_root = tmp_path / "repo"
   642→    repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   643→    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-wt"
   644→    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   645→
   646→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   647→        worktrees={
   648→            repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")],
   649→        },
   650→        trunk_branches={repo_root: "main"},
   651→    )
   652→
   653→    ctx = context_for_test(git=fake_git, cwd=repo_root)
   654→
   655→    # Call the helper
   656→    result = _ensure_branch_not_checked_out(ctx, repo_root=repo_root, branch="feature-branch")
   657→
   658→    # Should return the worktree path where branch was found and released
   659→    assert result is not None
   660→    assert result.resolve() == worktree_path.resolve()
   661→
   662→    # Should have checked out detached HEAD at trunk
   663→    assert len(fake_git.detached_checkouts) == 1
   664→    path, ref = fake_git.detached_checkouts[0]
   665→    assert path.resolve() == worktree_path.resolve()
   666→    assert ref == "main"
   667→

   804→def test_cleanup_and_navigate_outputs_noop_script_when_not_current_branch(
   805→    tmp_path: Path,
   806→    capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str],
   807→) -> None:
   808→    """Test that --script outputs a no-op script when landing from a different directory.
   809→
   810→    Regression test for bug where land.sh wrapper failed with 'cat: : No such file or
   811→    directory' when landing a PR from a directory other than the branch's worktree.
   812→
   813→    Root cause: _cleanup_and_navigate() only outputs a script path when is_current_branch
   814→    is True. When False, it calls raise SystemExit(0) without outputting anything, causing
   815→    `cat ""` to fail in the land.sh wrapper.
   816→
   817→    Fix: When is_current_branch is False and script is True, output a no-op activation
   818→    script that maintains the contract that --script always outputs a valid script path.
   819→    """
   820→    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-branch"
   821→    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   822→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
   823→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
   824→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   825→    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"
   826→
   827→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   828→        worktrees={main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]},
   829→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   830→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   831→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
   832→        existing_paths={worktree_path, main_repo_root, main_repo_root / ".git"},
   833→    )
   834→
   835→    # Create a FakeScriptWriter to verify script content
   836→    fake_script_writer = FakeScriptWriter()
   837→
   838→    # We're landing from main_repo_root, not from the branch's worktree
   839→    ctx = context_for_test(
   840→        git=fake_git,
   841→        graphite=GraphiteDisabled(reason=GraphiteDisabledReason.CONFIG_DISABLED),
   842→        script_writer=fake_script_writer,
   843→        cwd=main_repo_root,  # Not the branch's worktree
   844→    )
   845→
   846→    repo = RepoContext(
   847→        root=main_repo_root,
   848→        repo_name="test-repo",
   849→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   850→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   851→        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
   852→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   853→    )
   854→
   855→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with script=True but is_current_branch=False
   856→    # This simulates landing a PR by URL from master (not in the branch's worktree)
   857→    try:
   858→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   859→            ctx=ctx,
   860→            repo=repo,
   861→            branch="feature-branch",
   862→            worktree_path=None,  # No worktree cleanup needed
   863→            script=True,  # Key: requesting script output
   864→            pull_flag=False,
   865→            force=True,
   866→            is_current_branch=False,  # Key: not in branch's worktree
   867→            target_child_branch=None,
   868→            no_delete=False,
   869→            skip_activation_output=False,
   870→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   871→        )
   872→    except SystemExit as e:
   873→        assert e.code == 0  # Should exit cleanly
   874→
   875→    # Capture stdout where machine_output writes to
   876→    captured = capsys.readouterr()
   877→
   878→    # Verify a script path was output (not empty)
   879→    # The key assertion: --script should ALWAYS output a path, even when no navigation needed
   880→    script_path_output = captured.out.strip()
   881→    assert script_path_output, "Expected script path output, got empty string"
   882→
   883→    # Verify the script content contains the no-op marker
   884→    # Use FakeScriptWriter's stored content instead of reading from disk
   885→    assert fake_script_writer.last_script is not None, "No script was written"
   886→    script_content = fake_script_writer.last_script.content
   887→    assert "land complete" in script_content.lower(), (
   888→        f"Expected 'land complete' in script content, got: {script_content[:200]}"
   889→    )
   890→

   892→def test_cleanup_and_navigate_skip_activation_output_with_up_flag(
   893→    tmp_path: Path,
   894→) -> None:
   895→    """Test that skip_activation_output=True skips activation in --up mode.
   896→
   897→    Regression test for bug where duplicate activation messages appeared when
   898→    using `erk land --up` in execute mode. The bug was that the skip_activation_output
   899→    check was only in the else branch (non-up mode) but not in the if branch (--up mode).
   900→
   901→    The fix adds skip_activation_output check at the start of the --up branch.
   902→    """
   903→    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-branch"
   904→    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   905→    child_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "child-branch"
   906→    child_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
   907→    main_repo_root = tmp_path
   908→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
   909→
   910→    fake_git = FakeGit(
   911→        worktrees={
   912→            main_repo_root: [
   913→                WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
   914→                WorktreeInfo(path=child_worktree_path, branch="child-branch"),
   915→            ]
   916→        },
   917→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
   918→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
   919→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch", "child-branch"]},
   920→        existing_paths={
   921→            worktree_path,
   922→            child_worktree_path,
   923→            main_repo_root,
   924→            main_repo_root / ".git",
   925→        },
   926→    )
   927→
   928→    ctx = context_for_test(
   929→        git=fake_git,
   930→        graphite=GraphiteDisabled(reason=GraphiteDisabledReason.CONFIG_DISABLED),
   931→        cwd=worktree_path,
   932→    )
   933→
   934→    repo = RepoContext(
   935→        root=main_repo_root,
   936→        repo_name="test-repo",
   937→        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
   938→        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
   939→        pool_json_path=main_repo_root / "pool.json",
   940→        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
   941→    )
   942→
   943→    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with skip_activation_output=True and target_child_branch
   944→    # This simulates the execute mode (from sourcing land.sh) with --up flag
   945→    try:
   946→        _cleanup_and_navigate(
   947→            ctx=ctx,
   948→            repo=repo,
   949→            branch="feature-branch",
   950→            worktree_path=None,  # No worktree to clean up
   951→            script=False,
   952→            pull_flag=False,
   953→            force=True,
   954→            is_current_branch=False,
   955→            target_child_branch="child-branch",  # --up mode
   956→            no_delete=False,
   957→            skip_activation_output=True,  # Execute mode - should skip activation
   958→            cleanup_confirmed=True,
   959→        )
   960→    except SystemExit as e:
   961→        assert e.code == 0  # Expected - exits cleanly without activation output
   962→
   963→    # Verify that no worktree activation happened (no checkouts to child branch worktree)
   964→    # The key assertion: with skip_activation_output=True, we should exit immediately
   965→    # without calling find_worktree_for_branch or activate_worktree
   966→    assert len(fake_git.checked_out_branches) == 0, (
   967→        "Should not have checked out any branches when skip_activation_output=True"
   968→    )

  1066→def test_cleanup_no_worktree_ensures_branch_not_checked_out_before_delete(
  1067→    tmp_path: Path,
  1068→) -> None:
  1069→    """Test that NO_WORKTREE path releases branch before deletion.
  1070→
  1071→    Regression test for bug where _cleanup_no_worktree skipped the
  1072→    _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() call, causing delete_branch() to fail
  1073→    when the branch was still checked out in another worktree.
  1074→
  1075→    Scenario:
  1076→    - worktree_path=None (NO_WORKTREE classification)
  1077→    - Branch is checked out in an implementation worktree
  1078→    - Without fix: delete_branch() fails because branch is still checked out
  1079→    - With fix: _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() detaches HEAD first
  1080→    """
  1081→    other_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
  1082→    other_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
  1083→    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
  1084→    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
  1085→    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
  1086→
  1087→    # Branch is checked out in another worktree
  1088→    fake_git = FakeGit(
  1089→        worktrees={
  1090→            main_repo_root: [
  1091→                WorktreeInfo(path=other_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
  1092→            ]
  1093→        },
  1094→        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
  1095→        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
  1096→        trunk_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
  1097→        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
  1098→        existing_paths={
  1099→            other_worktree_path,
  1100→            main_repo_root,
  1101→            main_repo_root / ".git",
  1102→        },

"""Unit tests for _cleanup_and_navigate and related helpers."""

from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path

import pytest

from erk.cli.commands.land_cmd import (
    _cleanup_and_navigate,
    _ensure_branch_not_checked_out,
)
from erk.cli.ensure import UserFacingCliError
from erk.core.context import context_for_test
from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext
from erk.core.worktree_pool import PoolState, SlotAssignment, load_pool_state, save_pool_state
from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.disabled import GraphiteDisabled, GraphiteDisabledReason
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata
from tests.fakes.script_writer import FakeScriptWriter


def _create_test_assignment(
    slot_name: str,
    branch_name: str,
    worktree_path: Path,
) -> SlotAssignment:
    """Create a test assignment with current timestamp."""
    return SlotAssignment(
        slot_name=slot_name,
        branch_name=branch_name,
        assigned_at=datetime.now(UTC).isoformat(),
        worktree_path=worktree_path,
    )


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_uses_plain_git_delete_when_graphite_disabled(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that _cleanup_and_navigate uses git.delete_branch when Graphite is disabled."""
    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-branch"
    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]},
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={worktree_path, main_repo_root, main_repo_root / ".git"},
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=GraphiteDisabled(reason=GraphiteDisabledReason.CONFIG_DISABLED),
        cwd=worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=main_repo_root / "pool.json",
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=None,  # No worktree to handle, just branch deletion
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify branch was deleted via plain git (not graphite)
    assert "feature-branch" in fake_git.deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_detects_slot_by_branch_name(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Test that slot detection uses branch name, not worktree path.

    Regression test for bug where slot worktrees were not detected because
    path comparison failed. The fix uses find_branch_assignment() instead of
    find_assignment_by_worktree_path() to match by branch name.
    """
    # Use different paths to simulate path mismatch scenario
    # In the bug, pool.json stored one path but git reported a different path
    stored_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-a" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    actual_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-b" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    stored_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    actual_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create pool state with assignment using stored path (different from actual)
    assignment = _create_test_assignment(
        slot_name="erk-slot-01",
        branch_name="feature-branch",
        worktree_path=stored_worktree_path,  # Stored path differs from actual
    )

    initial_state = PoolState.test(assignments=(assignment,))
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, initial_state)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=actual_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            actual_worktree_path,
            stored_worktree_path,  # Assignment uses this path
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=actual_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with the actual path (which differs from stored)
    # The bug would cause this to NOT detect the slot and delete the worktree
    # The fix should detect the slot by branch name and unassign instead
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=actual_worktree_path,  # Actual path differs from stored
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify slot was unassigned (detected as slot by branch name)
    reloaded_state = load_pool_state(pool_json_path)
    assert reloaded_state is not None
    # Assignment should be removed (slot unassigned)
    matching_assignments = [
        a for a in reloaded_state.assignments if a.branch_name == "feature-branch"
    ]
    assert len(matching_assignments) == 0, "Slot should have been unassigned"

    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_detects_slot_by_path_pattern_without_assignment(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test slot detection by worktree path pattern when no pool assignment exists.

    Regression test for bug where slot worktrees (e.g., erk-slot-01) were deleted
    when branches were checked out via 'gt get' instead of erk commands.

    The bug occurred because:
    1. 'gt get' checks out a branch without creating a pool.json assignment
    2. _cleanup_and_navigate only checked pool.json for slot detection
    3. With no assignment, it fell through to "delete worktree" path

    The fix adds a fallback: if no assignment but worktree path matches erk-slot-XX
    pattern, treat it as a slot and release (don't delete the worktree directory).
    """
    # Create a slot worktree without any pool assignment
    slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create empty pool state (no assignments)

    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    # Create the placeholder branch that should exist
    placeholder_branch = "__erk-slot-01-br-stub__"

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch", placeholder_branch]},
        existing_paths={
            slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
    # Without the fix: would delete the worktree (bad!)
    # With the fix: should detect slot by path pattern and release it (good!)
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=slot_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify worktree was NOT deleted (key assertion!)
    # The worktree should still exist in git's worktree list
    assert slot_worktree_path not in fake_git.removed_worktrees

    # Verify placeholder branch was checked out
    checkout_calls = [
        (path, branch)
        for path, branch in fake_git.checked_out_branches
        if branch == placeholder_branch
    ]
    assert len(checkout_calls) == 1, "Should have checked out placeholder branch"
    assert checkout_calls[0][0] == slot_worktree_path

    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_non_slot_worktree_removes_worktree_after_branch_deletion(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that non-slot worktree cleanup removes the worktree after deleting the branch.

    Non-slot worktrees have no useful state after branch deletion (unlike slot
    worktrees which have placeholder branches). The worktree should be removed
    entirely to avoid leaving a dead detached-HEAD directory.
    """
    # Create a non-slot worktree (name doesn't match erk-slot-XX pattern)
    non_slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "my-feature-worktree"
    non_slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create empty pool state (no slot assignments)

    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=non_slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            non_slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch so GraphiteBranchManager uses Graphite delete
    # (GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch does LBYL check before calling graphite.delete_branch)
    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=non_slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
    # Without the fix: would fail because branch is checked out
    # With the fix: should checkout trunk first, then delete branch
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=non_slot_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify detached HEAD at trunk was checked out before deletion
    # (we use detached HEAD because trunk may be checked out in root worktree)
    detached_calls = [(path, ref) for path, ref in fake_git.detached_checkouts if ref == "main"]
    assert len(detached_calls) == 1, "Should have checked out detached HEAD at trunk"
    assert detached_calls[0][0] == non_slot_worktree_path

    # Verify worktree WAS removed (non-slot worktrees are removed after branch deletion)
    assert non_slot_worktree_path in fake_git.removed_worktrees

    # Verify safe_chdir was called to escape the worktree before removal
    assert len(fake_git.chdir_history) > 0, "Should have called safe_chdir before removal"

    # Verify branch was deleted via Graphite (since FakeGraphite is used and branch is tracked)
    # GraphiteBranchManager.delete_branch calls graphite.delete_branch when branch is tracked
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_non_slot_worktree_fails_with_uncommitted_changes(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that non-slot worktree cleanup fails if there are uncommitted changes.

    Before switching to trunk, we must check for uncommitted changes to prevent
    accidental loss of work.
    """
    # Create a non-slot worktree
    non_slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "my-feature-worktree"
    non_slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create empty pool state

    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=non_slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            non_slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
        # Simulate uncommitted changes in the worktree (modified files)
        file_statuses={non_slot_worktree_path: ([], ["modified_file.py"], [])},
    )

    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite()

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=non_slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate - should fail with uncommitted changes
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=non_slot_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
        pytest.fail("Expected UserFacingCliError for uncommitted changes")
    except UserFacingCliError:
        pass  # Expected

    # Verify no checkout was attempted
    assert len(fake_git.checked_out_branches) == 0

    # Verify branch was NOT deleted
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" not in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_ensures_branch_not_checked_out_before_delete_with_stale_pool_state(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that cleanup verifies branch is released before deletion.

    Regression test for bug where delete fails when pool state's worktree_path
    is stale (doesn't match the actual worktree location).

    Scenario:
    - Pool state has assignment with worktree_path = stale_path
    - Branch is actually checked out in actual_path (different from stale_path)
    - execute_unassign() checkouts placeholder at stale_path (wrong location)
    - Without fix: delete_branch() fails because branch still checked out in actual_path
    - With fix: _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() detects and releases the branch

    The fix adds a defensive check that finds the branch wherever it's checked out
    and releases it before deletion.
    """
    # Two different paths to simulate stale pool state
    stale_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-stale" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    actual_worktree_path = tmp_path / "erk-root-actual" / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    stale_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    actual_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create pool state with assignment using STALE path (different from actual)
    assignment = _create_test_assignment(
        slot_name="erk-slot-01",
        branch_name="feature-branch",
        worktree_path=stale_worktree_path,  # STALE - differs from actual
    )

    initial_state = PoolState.test(assignments=(assignment,))
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, initial_state)

    # Create FakeGit where branch is checked out at ACTUAL path
    # This simulates the stale pool state scenario
    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [
                WorktreeInfo(path=actual_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
            ]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        trunk_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            actual_worktree_path,
            stale_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch
    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=actual_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate
    # The bug would have failed here because branch is still checked out in actual_path
    # The fix ensures branch is released before deletion
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=actual_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify the defensive checkout_detached was called on ACTUAL path
    # (This is the key assertion - the fix finds where branch is actually checked out)
    detached_calls = [(path, ref) for path, ref in fake_git.detached_checkouts if ref == "main"]
    # Should have at least one detached checkout at actual_worktree_path
    actual_path_detached = [
        (path, ref)
        for path, ref in detached_calls
        if path.resolve() == actual_worktree_path.resolve()
    ]
    assert len(actual_path_detached) >= 1, (
        f"Expected detached checkout at actual_worktree_path. "
        f"Got detached_checkouts: {fake_git.detached_checkouts}"
    )

    # Verify branch was deleted successfully
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_ensure_branch_not_checked_out_helper_releases_branch(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
    """Test that _ensure_branch_not_checked_out helper correctly releases a branch.

    This tests the helper function directly to verify it:
    1. Finds the worktree where the branch is checked out
    2. Checkouts detached HEAD at trunk to release the branch
    3. Returns the path where detachment happened
    """
    repo_root = tmp_path / "repo"
    repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-wt"
    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")],
        },
        trunk_branches={repo_root: "main"},
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(git=fake_git, cwd=repo_root)

    # Call the helper
    result = _ensure_branch_not_checked_out(ctx, repo_root=repo_root, branch="feature-branch")

    # Should return the worktree path where branch was found and released
    assert result is not None
    assert result.resolve() == worktree_path.resolve()

    # Should have checked out detached HEAD at trunk
    assert len(fake_git.detached_checkouts) == 1
    path, ref = fake_git.detached_checkouts[0]
    assert path.resolve() == worktree_path.resolve()
    assert ref == "main"

def test_cleanup_and_navigate_slot_without_assignment_force_suppresses_warning(
    tmp_path: Path,
    capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str],
) -> None:
    """Test that warning is suppressed when force=True for slot without assignment.

    When running `erk land -f` from a slot worktree without assignment, the warning
    should NOT be displayed since --force was specified.
    """

    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    # Create empty pool state (no assignments)
    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    # Create the placeholder branch that should exist
    placeholder_branch = "__erk-slot-01-br-stub__"

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch", placeholder_branch]},
        existing_paths={
            slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    # Configure FakeGraphite to track the branch
    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with force=True
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,

            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,  # This should suppress the warning
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Capture stderr where user_output writes to

    # Verify warning was NOT printed (suppressed by force=True)

    # Verify the operation still completed successfully
    # Placeholder branch should have been checked out
    checkout_calls = [
        (path, branch)
        for path, branch in fake_git.checked_out_branches
        if branch == placeholder_branch

    assert len(checkout_calls) == 1, "Should have checked out placeholder branch"

    # Branch should have been deleted
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_outputs_noop_script_when_not_current_branch(
    tmp_path: Path,
    capsys: pytest.CaptureFixture[str],
) -> None:
    """Test that --script outputs a no-op script when landing from a different directory.

    Regression test for bug where land.sh wrapper failed with 'cat: : No such file or
    directory' when landing a PR from a directory other than the branch's worktree.

    Root cause: _cleanup_and_navigate() only outputs a script path when is_current_branch
    is True. When False, it calls raise SystemExit(0) without outputting anything, causing
    `cat ""` to fail in the land.sh wrapper.

    Fix: When is_current_branch is False and script is True, output a no-op activation
    script that maintains the contract that --script always outputs a valid script path.
    """
    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-branch"
    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]},
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={worktree_path, main_repo_root, main_repo_root / ".git"},
    )

    # Create a FakeScriptWriter to verify script content
    fake_script_writer = FakeScriptWriter()

    # We're landing from main_repo_root, not from the branch's worktree
    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=GraphiteDisabled(reason=GraphiteDisabledReason.CONFIG_DISABLED),
        script_writer=fake_script_writer,
        cwd=main_repo_root,  # Not the branch's worktree
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with script=True but is_current_branch=False
    # This simulates landing a PR by URL from master (not in the branch's worktree)
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=None,  # No worktree cleanup needed
            script=True,  # Key: requesting script output
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,  # Key: not in branch's worktree
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit as e:
        assert e.code == 0  # Should exit cleanly

    # Capture stdout where machine_output writes to
    captured = capsys.readouterr()

    # Verify a script path was output (not empty)
    # The key assertion: --script should ALWAYS output a path, even when no navigation needed
    script_path_output = captured.out.strip()
    assert script_path_output, "Expected script path output, got empty string"

    # Verify the script content contains the no-op marker
    # Use FakeScriptWriter's stored content instead of reading from disk
    assert fake_script_writer.last_script is not None, "No script was written"
    script_content = fake_script_writer.last_script.content
    assert "land complete" in script_content.lower(), (
        f"Expected 'land complete' in script content, got: {script_content[:200]}"
    )


def test_cleanup_and_navigate_skip_activation_output_with_up_flag(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that skip_activation_output=True skips activation in --up mode.

    Regression test for bug where duplicate activation messages appeared when
    using `erk land --up` in execute mode. The bug was that the skip_activation_output
    check was only in the else branch (non-up mode) but not in the if branch (--up mode).

    The fix adds skip_activation_output check at the start of the --up branch.
    """
    worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "feature-branch"
    worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    child_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "child-branch"
    child_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [
                WorktreeInfo(path=worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
                WorktreeInfo(path=child_worktree_path, branch="child-branch"),
            ]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch", "child-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            worktree_path,
            child_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=GraphiteDisabled(reason=GraphiteDisabledReason.CONFIG_DISABLED),
        cwd=worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=main_repo_root / "pool.json",
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # Call _cleanup_and_navigate with skip_activation_output=True and target_child_branch
    # This simulates the execute mode (from sourcing land.sh) with --up flag
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=None,  # No worktree to clean up
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch="child-branch",  # --up mode
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=True,  # Execute mode - should skip activation
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit as e:
        assert e.code == 0  # Expected - exits cleanly without activation output

    # Verify that no worktree activation happened (no checkouts to child branch worktree)
    # The key assertion: with skip_activation_output=True, we should exit immediately
    # without calling find_worktree_for_branch or activate_worktree
    assert len(fake_git.checked_out_branches) == 0, (
        "Should not have checked out any branches when skip_activation_output=True"
    )

def test_cleanup_no_worktree_ensures_branch_not_checked_out_before_delete(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that NO_WORKTREE path releases branch before deletion.

    Regression test for bug where _cleanup_no_worktree skipped the
    _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() call, causing delete_branch() to fail
    when the branch was still checked out in another worktree.

    Scenario:
    - worktree_path=None (NO_WORKTREE classification)
    - Branch is checked out in an implementation worktree
    - Without fix: delete_branch() fails because branch is still checked out
    - With fix: _ensure_branch_not_checked_out() detaches HEAD first
    """
    other_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "erk-slot-01"
    other_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()

    # Branch is checked out in another worktree
    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [
                WorktreeInfo(path=other_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch"),
            ]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        trunk_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            other_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
        },
    )

    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=main_repo_root,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=main_repo_root / "pool.json",
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    # worktree_path=None triggers NO_WORKTREE classification
    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=None,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected - function raises SystemExit(0) at end

    # Verify _ensure_branch_not_checked_out detached HEAD in the other worktree
    detached_calls = [(path, ref) for path, ref in fake_git.detached_checkouts if ref == "main"]
    actual_path_detached = [
        (path, ref)
        for path, ref in detached_calls
        if path.resolve() == other_worktree_path.resolve()
    ]
    assert len(actual_path_detached) >= 1, (
        f"Expected detached checkout at other_worktree_path. "
        f"Got detached_checkouts: {fake_git.detached_checkouts}"
    )

    # Verify branch was deleted successfully
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" in deleted_branches


def test_cleanup_non_slot_worktree_preserves_both_when_not_confirmed(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that cleanup_confirmed=False preserves both branch and worktree.

    When the user declines the confirmation prompt, no cleanup should occur:
    the branch should remain and the worktree should not be removed.
    """
    non_slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "my-feature-worktree"
    non_slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=non_slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            non_slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=non_slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=non_slot_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=False,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=False,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected

    # Branch should NOT be deleted
    deleted_branches = [branch for _path, branch in fake_graphite.delete_branch_calls]
    assert "feature-branch" not in deleted_branches

    # Worktree should NOT be removed
    assert non_slot_worktree_path not in fake_git.removed_worktrees

    # No detached checkouts should have occurred
    assert len(fake_git.detached_checkouts) == 0

    # No safe_chdir should have occurred
    assert len(fake_git.chdir_history) == 0


def test_cleanup_non_slot_worktree_calls_safe_chdir_before_removal(
    tmp_path: Path,
) -> None:
    """Test that safe_chdir escapes the worktree cwd before git worktree remove.

    git worktree remove fails if the process cwd is inside the worktree being
    removed. safe_chdir must be called to move the cwd to the main repo root
    before the removal call.
    """
    non_slot_worktree_path = tmp_path / "worktrees" / "my-feature-worktree"
    non_slot_worktree_path.mkdir(parents=True)
    main_repo_root = tmp_path / "main-repo"
    main_repo_root.mkdir(parents=True)
    (main_repo_root / ".git").mkdir()
    pool_json_path = main_repo_root / "pool.json"

    empty_state = PoolState.test(assignments=())
    save_pool_state(pool_json_path, empty_state)

    fake_git = FakeGit(
        worktrees={
            main_repo_root: [WorktreeInfo(path=non_slot_worktree_path, branch="feature-branch")]
        },
        git_common_dirs={main_repo_root: main_repo_root / ".git"},
        default_branches={main_repo_root: "main"},
        local_branches={main_repo_root: ["main", "feature-branch"]},
        existing_paths={
            non_slot_worktree_path,
            main_repo_root,
            main_repo_root / ".git",
            pool_json_path,
        },
    )

    fake_graphite = FakeGraphite(
        branches={
            "feature-branch": BranchMetadata(
                name="feature-branch",
                parent="main",
                children=[],
                is_trunk=False,
                commit_sha=None,
            ),
        },
    )

    ctx = context_for_test(
        git=fake_git,
        graphite=fake_graphite,
        cwd=non_slot_worktree_path,
    )

    repo = RepoContext(
        root=main_repo_root,
        repo_name="test-repo",
        repo_dir=main_repo_root,
        worktrees_dir=tmp_path / "worktrees",
        pool_json_path=pool_json_path,
        github=GitHubRepoId(owner="owner", repo="repo"),
    )

    try:
        _cleanup_and_navigate(
            ctx=ctx,
            repo=repo,
            branch="feature-branch",
            worktree_path=non_slot_worktree_path,
            script=False,
            pull_flag=False,
            force=True,
            is_current_branch=True,
            target_child_branch=None,
            no_delete=False,
            skip_activation_output=False,
            cleanup_confirmed=True,
        )
    except SystemExit:
        pass  # Expected

    # Verify safe_chdir was called to escape the worktree before removal
    assert len(fake_git.chdir_history) > 0, "Should have called safe_chdir before worktree removal"
    assert fake_git.chdir_history[0] == main_repo_root, (
        "safe_chdir should navigate to main repo root"
    )

    # Verify worktree was removed after safe_chdir
    assert non_slot_worktree_path in fake_git.removed_worktrees
